---

## Follow-up queries for `websearch`

Adds a `--previous-query` flag on `chunkhound websearch` and a matching `previous_query` parameter on the `websearch` MCP tool. When set, two things change: (1) the LLM query-expansion prompt switches to a follow-up branch that steers the 3 DDG variants toward *new* dimensions instead of re-exploring the same ground; (2) the synthesis engine reframes the answer in the prior topic's context via a new `ResearchContext.previous_query`. Every response now closes with a `"Continue exploring:"` footer carrying a runnable follow-up incantation — shell form on the CLI, `websearch(query: …, previous_query: …)` on MCP.

Two supporting mechanisms are worth flagging. First, `expand_web_queries()` now returns `WebExpansionResult(queries, search_query)`: the LLM emits its own normalized form of the input, which becomes the `_quickresearch` positional and the footer's copy-paste payload (falling back to the raw query on any failure). Second, `deep_research_impl` accepts `previous_query` via `Annotated[..., InternalOnly()]` — a new schema-generator marker that keeps the parameter out of the `code_research` MCP tool's `tools/list` schema, so the chaining hook flows through the internal `_quickresearch` subprocess without leaking to public schemas or the `chunkhound research` CLI. Prompt text was also reorganized: the expansion prompt uses `<query>` / `<previous_query>` XML tag delimiters (so quoted input phrases don't produce ambiguous `""..."" ` sequences), and the follow-up examples block was hoisted from `utils/websearch_expansion.py` into `services/prompts/websearch_expansion.py` alongside the other prompt constants.

Value: follow-up questions in a research chain stop being "redo the first search" and instead build on it. No breaking changes — `previous_query` defaults to `None` at every surface, the `code_research` schema is unchanged, and non-follow-up calls take the baseline path unchanged. Tests cover expansion contract (pad/truncate, quote preservation, follow-up prompt selection), the `InternalOnly` schema hiding both generically and on `deep_research_impl`, subprocess argv plumbing, empty-string → `None` coercion at each surface, and per-surface footer format.
